channel.title: U.S. Police Shooting Research Tracker
channel.description: Recent academic research on police shootings and use of deadly force

item.title: Racial and ethnic disparities in fatal police shootings: Variation across U.S. states and the role of firearm ownership.
item.description:
Authors: Neil R, Bushway S, Schell TL et al.
Journal: PLoS One

item.title: Comparing police officers with and without strangulation training on perceptions of strangulation as a risk for intimate partner violence and the importance of medical-forensic exams
item.description:
Authors: McCormick et al.
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose Strangulation is one of the strongest predictors of future intimate partner femicide. As first responders who may be the only point of contact for a survivor, it is imperative that police officers are trained to recognize the signs and symptoms of strangulation, particularly because few survivors show visible injuries and many do not seek healthcare. The current study assessed strangulation knowledge amon...

item.title: Examining police responses to stalking and their effectiveness using national data
item.description:
Authors: Ngo et al.
Journal: International Journal of Police Science & Management

Stalking remains a pervasive public safety problem, yet limited research has examined the factors associated with police responses and how these responses are related to victim-reported outcomes. Using nationally representative data from the 2016 and 2019 Stalking Victimization Supplements of the National Crime Victimization Survey, this study examined the correlates of ten police actions and assessed the associations between specific police responses and whether victims reported that st...
item.source: International Journal of Police Science & Management

item.title: The Impact of Prior Military Experience and Burnout on Police Officer Use of Force
item.description:
Authors: Fiske & Schriver
Journal: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology
item.source: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

item.title: Cumulative Exposure to Police-Involved Shootings and Mental Health Conditions in Minneapolis, Minnesota
item.description:
Authors: Tanveer et al.
Journal: Journal of Urban Health

item.title: Police body-worn cameras and fatal police shootings: A national quasi-experimental study of aggregate effects and treatment effect heterogeneity
item.description:
Authors: Sheppard, Keller G.
Journal: Journal of Criminal Justice
